basicDetails.implementingAgency: Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The &quot;Financial Assistance for Marriage&quot; scheme is implemented by the Bihar Building &amp; Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BBOCWWB), Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹50,000/- is provided to registered male or female workers who have been members of the Board for at least three years, for the marriage of their two adult daughters and for the female member&#39;s own marriage. Workers who remarry are not eligible for this scheme. This assistance is in addition to the benefits provided under the “[Antarjatiye Vivah Protsahan Anudan Yojana](https://www.myscheme.gov.in/schemes/avpay)”.
schemeContent.benefits_md: Under this scheme, registered male or female workers receive financial assistance of ₹50,000/- for the marriage of their two adult daughters and for the marriage of the female member herself.
schemeContent.briefDescription: Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹50,000/- is provided to registered male or female workers who have been members of the Board for at least three years, for the marriage of their two adult daughters and for the female member's own marriage.

children.text: The "Financial Assistance for Marriage" scheme is implemented by the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (BBOCWWB), Labour Resources Department, Government of Bihar. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹50,000/- is provided to registered male or female workers who have been members of the Board for at least three years, for the marriage of their two adult daughters and for the female member's own marriage. Workers who remarry are not eligible for this scheme. This assistance is in addition to the benefits provided under the “
Antarjatiye Vivah Protsahan Anudan Yojana
Under this scheme, registered male or female workers receive financial assistance of ₹50,000/- for the marriage of their two adult daughters and for the marriage of the female member herself.
Registration of a Construction Worker under BBOCWWB:
Step 1:
Visit the
Official Website
of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step 2:
On the homepage, click on "Labour Registration".
Step 3:
If you are not registered, click on "
Apply for New Registration
Step 4:
Enter your Aadhar card number and name, then click "Authenticate".
Step 5:
A dashboard will appear. Enter all required details, ensuring that all fields marked with an asterisk (*) are filled in correctly.
Step 6:
Click on "Submit" and proceed to pay the application fee online.
Step 7:
You will receive your construction worker registration number upon successful registration.
Apply for Scheme Benefits:
Step 1:
Visit the
Official Website
of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
Step 2:
Click on "Scheme Application" and select "
Apply for Scheme
Step 3:
Enter your registration number and click "Show" to view your details.
Step 4:
Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail the benefits.
Step 5:
Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents.
Step 6:
Click on "Submit Application" to complete the process.
Application Tracking:
Applicant can track their application status online through the
official website
of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Registration of a Construction Worker under BBOCWWB:**

**Step 1:** Visit the [Official Website](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/LabourReg.aspx) of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** On the homepage, click on &quot;Labour Registration&quot;.
**Step 3:** If you are not registered, click on &quot;[Apply for New Registration](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/AddLabourRegistrationWeb.aspx)&quot;.
**Step 4:** Enter your Aadhar card number and name, then click &quot;Authenticate&quot;.
**Step 5:** A dashboard will appear. Enter all required details, ensuring that all fields marked with an asterisk (*) are filled in correctly.
**Step 6:** Click on &quot;Submit&quot; and proceed to pay the application fee online.
**Step 7:** You will receive your construction worker registration number upon successful registration.
> **Apply for Scheme Benefits:**

**Step 1:** Visit the [Official Website](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Website/SchemeApply.aspx) of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
**Step 2:** Click on &quot;Scheme Application&quot; and select &quot;[Apply for Scheme](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/SchemeTaggingWeb.aspx)&quot;.
**Step 3:** Enter your registration number and click &quot;Show&quot; to view your details.
**Step 4:** Select the scheme you wish to apply for to avail the benefits.
**Step 5:** Enter all required information and upload the necessary documents.
**Step 6:** Click on &quot;Submit Application&quot; to complete the process. 
> **Application Tracking:**

Applicant can track their application status online through the [official website](https://bocw.bihar.gov.in/Registration/ApplicationStatus.aspx) of the Bihar Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. Relevant updates will be sent to the registered mobile number.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
   1. The worker should be registered with the Bihar Building &amp; Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
   1. The worker should have maintained continuous membership with the Board for at least three years.
   1. The worker&#39;s membership should be active at the time of application.
   1. Both male and female registered workers can avail benefits for the marriage of their two adult daughters, while female workers are also eligible for assistance for their own marriage.
   1. Workers who remarry are not eligible for this scheme.

Note: This financial assistance is in addition to benefits provided under the “Antarjatiye Vivah Protsahan Anudan Yojana”.

children.text: The worker should be engaged in building or construction work.
The worker should be registered with the Bihar Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
The worker should have maintained continuous membership with the Board for at least three years.
The worker's membership should be active at the time of application.
Both male and female registered workers can avail benefits for the marriage of their two adult daughters, while female workers are also eligible for assistance for their own marriage.
Workers who remarry are not eligible for this scheme.
Note: This financial assistance is in addition to benefits provided under the “Antarjatiye Vivah Protsahan Anudan Yojana”.
